title: Jejunal/Ileal Atresia: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Jejunal/Ileal Atresia
Identify key diagnostic features of Jejunal/Ileal Atresia
Apply clinical reasoning to Jejunal/Ileal Atresia management
Recognize common pitfalls in Jejunal/Ileal Atresia

sections.title: Overview of Jejunal/Ileal Atresia
sections.content:
**Jejunal/Ileal Atresia**

Vascular accident theory, apple-peel atresia, triple bubble sign, surgical management

**Key Points:**
• Caused by in-utero mesenteric vascular accident (NOT failed recanalization like duodenal atresia)
• Types: I (membrane), II (fibrous cord), IIIa (V-shaped mesentery gap), IIIb (apple-peel/Christmas tree), IV (multiple)
• X-ray: dilated proximal loops, air-fluid levels, no distal gas

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Caused by in-utero mesenteric vascular accident (NOT failed recanalization like duodenal atresia)
• Types: I (membrane), II (fibrous cord), IIIa (V-shaped mesentery gap), IIIb (apple-peel/Christmas tree), IV (multiple)

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• X-ray: dilated proximal loops, air-fluid levels, no distal gas
• Contrast enema: microcolon (unused)
• Treatment: surgical resection and primary anastomosis

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Jejunal/Ileal Atresia:**

• X-ray: dilated proximal loops, air-fluid levels, no distal gas
• Contrast enema: microcolon (unused)
• Treatment: surgical resection and primary anastomosis
